The test took place…

items.title: Scientists solved the mystery of missing ocean plastic—and the answer is alarming
items.summary: Scientists have discovered that the ocean’s “missing” plastic hasn’t vanished—it has broken down into trillions of invisible nanoplastics now spread through water, air, and living organisms. These tiny particles may be everywhere, including inside our bodies, raising serious concerns about their impact.

items.title: Scientists just found a way to store massive data using light in 3 dimensions
items.summary: A new holographic storage technique uses light in three dimensions to dramatically increase how much data can be stored. It encodes information throughout a material using amplitude, phase, and polarization, rather than just on a surface. An AI model then reconstructs the data from light patterns, simplifying the process. This could pave the way for faster, denser, and more efficient data storage systems.

items.title: Popular sugar substitute linked to brain damage and stroke risk
items.summary: A widely used sugar substitute found in everything from keto snacks to diet drinks may not be as harmless as it seems. New research shows that erythritol can disrupt brain blood vessel cells, reducing their ability to relax, increasing harmful oxidative stress, and impairing the body’s ability to break down clots. These changes create conditions that could raise stroke risk, even at typical consumption levels.

items.title: How snakes defy gravity to stand tall
items.summary: Limbless tree snakes can lift most of their body into the air without toppling. They manage this by focusing all their bending forces at their base.

items.title: Welcome to the weird world of AI agent teams
items.summary: AI agents are starting to work in teams, but without careful organization, groups of bots can easily fall into chaos.

items.title: A rare star in a tiny galaxy preserves a record of the early universe
items.summary: Found in an ultrafaint dwarf galaxy, the ancient star’s unusual chemistry indicates it formed from gas enriched by a single early supernova.
